description: Add multi-language support (Uzbek latin / Russian) with /start language selection and /lang command. Store language preference in database. Create locales/ directory with all bot messages in both languages.
steps: Add a 'language' column (String, default 'uz') to the User model in database.py. Add functions: get_user_language(telegram_id) -> str and set_user_language(telegram_id, lang: str).

Create locales/ directory with two files: locales/uz.py and locales/ru.py. Each file exports a dictionary (MESSAGES) with keys for ALL bot strings: greetings, menu buttons, prompts, error messages, report templates, admin messages, etc. Extract every hardcoded string from main.py into these dictionaries.
Create locales/__init__.py that provides a get_text(key, lang='uz', **kwargs) function which fetches the string from the appropriate language dictionary and formats it with kwargs if provided.
Modify the /start command handler in main.py: when a new user starts, show a language selection inline keyboard with 'O`zbekcha' and 'Russkiy' buttons. Save the selection to the database.
Add a /lang command handler in main.py that shows the language selection keyboard at any time for changing language.

Update ALL handler functions in main.py to fetch the user's language from the database and use get_text() for all bot responses. This includes: main_kb and admin_kb (make them dynamic functions that accept a lang parameter), all message.answer() calls, all callback responses, the monthly reminder text, etc.

Make the ReplyKeyboardMarkup buttons language-dependent by creating a function get_main_kb(lang) that returns the keyboard with translated button text. Similarly for admin_kb. Update F.text filters to match both language variants using F.text.in_([uz_text, ru_text]) pattern.
acceptance_criteria: locales/uz.py exists with MESSAGES dict containing all bot strings in Uzbek
locales/ru.py exists with MESSAGES dict containing all bot strings in Russian
User model has 'language' column with default 'uz'
database.py has get_user_language() and set_user_language() functions
/start shows language selection for new users
/lang command exists and works for changing language
All handler functions use get_text() instead of hardcoded strings
All .py files pass Python syntax validation
verification: python3 -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('main.py').read()); ast.parse(open('database.py').read()); ast.parse(open('locales/__init__.py').read()); ast.parse(open('locales/uz.py').read()); ast.parse(open('locales/ru.py').read()); print('All syntax OK')"
python3 -c "from locales import get_text; assert get_text('start_welcome', 'uz'); assert get_text('start_welcome', 'ru'); print('Locales work')"
grep -q 'get_text' main.py && echo 'main.py uses get_text' || echo 'MISSING get_text usage'
grep -q 'language' database.py && echo 'language column exists' || echo 'MISSING language column'
grep -q '/lang' main.py && echo '/lang command exists' || echo 'MISSING /lang command'
findings: All verification checks pass. The admin_export_headers key stores a list rather than a string, which works because get_text() only calls .format() when kwargs are provided. The /lang command uses Command('lang') filter. Language selection callback handles both new and existing users.
